recent_coach_change: No recent change; Bubista has been in charge since 2020 and led the country to its first-ever World Cup.
qualification_path: Qualified for their first-ever World Cup by winning CAF qualifying Group D in October 2025.
key_players: Logan Costa (Villarreal) — centre-back, regarded as the country's best player; only player from a top-five European league but a major fitness gamble after an ACL injury
Ryan Mendes — captain, all-time leading scorer and record appearance-maker, leading the line
Steven Moreira (Columbus Crew) — 2024 MLS Defender of the Year
Carlos 'CJ' dos Santos (San Diego FC) — goalkeeper, former US youth international who switched allegiance
Kevin Pina — attacker, scored in the warm-up win over Serbia

injuries_doubts.issue: Ruptured ACL in pre-season (July 2025); returned only for a 13-minute LaLiga cameo before being named in the squad

squad_notes: First World Cup appearance for the nation of roughly 600,000 (third-smallest World Cup country). Squad is heavily diaspora-based (players born in France, Portugal, Netherlands, Ireland). Bubista kept the core that secured qualification; Logan Costa's inclusion despite minimal match fitness is the headline gamble. Carlos dos Santos is the only uncapped player.
warmup_assessment: Excellent and confidence-building: Cape Verde won both June warm-ups 3-0, beating Serbia in Lisbon on May 31, 2026 and Bermuda in Connecticut on June 6, 2026.
shape_verdict: The debutants arrive in buoyant form after two convincing 3-0 warm-up wins, though they are the lowest-ranked side in Group H and lean on an injury-doubtful Logan Costa at the back.

news.headline: Cape Verde beat Bermuda 3-0 in final World Cup warm-up
Cape Verde thrash Serbia 3-0 in Lisbon warm-up
Bubista names World Cup squad, takes gamble on injury-returning Logan Costa
